name: SWYER-JAMES SYNDROME
definition: MACLEOD SYNDROME = UNILATERAL LOBAR EMPHYSEMA = IDIOPATHIC UNILATERAL chronic complication of infectious obliterative bronchiolitis in infancy or childhood as early

demographics.Etiology: acute viral bronchiolitis in infancy / early childhood (adenovirus, RSV, mycoplasma)
demographics.Path: variant of postinfectious constrictive bronchiolitis with acute obliterative bronchiolitis,

dominant_finding: hyperlucency of one lung ← air trapping ← bronchiolar obstruction
distribution: small ipsilateral hilum ← diminuted hilar vessels + attenuated arteries)
discriminating_features: hyperlucency of one lung ← air trapping ← bronchiolar obstruction
diminished number + size of pulmonary vessels:
small ipsilateral hilum ← diminuted hilar vessels + attenuated arteries)
normal hyperexpanded hemithorax ← collateral air drift

general: hyperlucency of one lung ← air trapping ← bronchiolar obstruction
diminished number + size of pulmonary vessels:
small ipsilateral hilum ← diminuted hilar vessels + attenuated arteries)
normal hyperexpanded hemithorax ← collateral air drift
mild cylindrical bronchiectasis with paucity of bronchial subdivisions (cutoff at 4th-5th
areas of normal lung attenuation within hypoattenuating lung
diminished size of pulmonary vessels in hyperlucent areas
ANGIO: matched defects of perfusion + ventilation (with delayed washout) in hyperlucent regions
incomplete filling of ipsilateral peripheral bronchioles
dilated bronchi with sharply terminating segments
